text: Pelé scored over 1,000 career goals in official FIFA-recognised matches.
explanation: Pelé's 1,000+ figure includes friendlies and unofficial matches. FIFA-recognised career goals are lower.

text: A direct free kick can result in an own goal in football.
explanation: If a direct free kick enters the goal without touching another outfield player, it counts as an own goal against the defending team.

text: The offside rule requires a player to be involved in active play, not merely positioned beyond the last defender.
explanation: Modern interpretations judge offside on involvement in active play, not position alone.

text: You can be offside from a throw-in in football.
explanation: There is no offside offence directly from a throw-in.

text: A goalkeeper can hold the ball for a maximum of six seconds when it is in their hands.
explanation: Law 12 limits how long a goalkeeper may hold the ball to six seconds.

text: A penalty shootout begins with five kicks per team.
explanation: Teams take five kicks each initially, then sudden death if still tied.

text: Lionel Messi has won the FIFA World Cup.
explanation: Messi won the World Cup with Argentina in 2022.

text: Cristiano Ronaldo has won the FIFA World Cup.
explanation: Ronaldo has not won a World Cup with Portugal.

text: Real Madrid have won more UEFA Champions League titles than any other club.
explanation: Real Madrid hold the record for European Cup/Champions League titles.

text: The Premier League was founded in 1992.
explanation: The Premier League replaced the old First Division as the top flight in England in 1992.

text: A standard football match lasts 90 minutes of playing time in regulation.
explanation: Regulation time is two 45-minute halves unless otherwise stated.

text: Substituted players may return to the pitch in a standard football match.
explanation: In standard play, a substituted player cannot return except under specific competition rules such as concussion substitutions.

text: Argentina won the 2022 FIFA World Cup.
explanation: Argentina beat France on penalties in the 2022 final in Qatar.

text: France won the 2018 FIFA World Cup.
explanation: France defeated Croatia 4-2 in the 2018 final in Russia.

text: Erling Haaland plays for Manchester City.
explanation: Haaland joined Manchester City from Borussia Dortmund in 2022.

text: Harry Kane still plays for Tottenham Hotspur.
explanation: Kane joined Bayern Munich in 2023.

text: Kylian Mbappé joined Real Madrid in 2024.
explanation: Mbappé left Paris Saint-Germain and signed for Real Madrid in 2024.

text: AC Milan and Inter Milan share the same stadium.
explanation: Both clubs play home matches at the San Siro (Giuseppe Meazza).

text: Liverpool's home stadium is Anfield.
explanation: Anfield has been Liverpool's home since their formation.

text: The Ballon d'Or is awarded to the best male footballer in a calendar year.
explanation: France Football's Ballon d'Or honours the outstanding male footballer of the year.

text: Nigeria have won the FIFA World Cup.
explanation: Nigeria have never won the men's World Cup.

text: Nigeria won the men's Olympic football gold medal in 1996.
explanation: Nigeria's Dream Team won gold in Atlanta in 1996.

text: The offside rule applies in your own half of the pitch.
explanation: A player cannot be offside in their own half.

text: A throw-in cannot directly result in a goal.
explanation: A goal cannot be scored directly from a throw-in.

text: VAR reviews every offside decision automatically in football.
explanation: VAR intervenes for clear and obvious errors or serious missed incidents in defined situations.
